title: ficus laevigata subvar. subdepressa
intro: Ficus laevigata subvar. subdepressa, also known as the smooth-leaved banyan, is a subvariety of the Ficus laevigata tree native to Southeast Asia. Research suggests that the plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The leaves of the plant are said to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
